stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Patricia Grace is a Maori author from New Zealand.
  • Potiki is one of her most celebrated novels.
  • Grace published the novel in 1986.
  • It is about a Maori community resisting the development of their ancestral coastal land.
  • Some of Potiki’s themes also appear in Grace’s later novel Cousins.
stem: The student wants to introduce Patricia Grace and her novel Potiki to a new audience. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Patricia Grace wrote the novel Cousins after she wrote Potiki.
Some of the themes that appear in Patricia Grace’s Cousins also appear in Potiki.
In 1986, Maori author Patricia Grace published one of her most celebrated novels, Potiki, which is about a Maori community resisting the development of their ancestral coastal land.
Potiki, a celebrated novel published in 1986, depicts a Maori community resisting development of their ancestral coastal land.

rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. It introduces Grace as a Maori author and identifies Potiki with its publication year and central subject, providing the background needed for a new audience.

Choice A discusses publication order rather than introducing the author or the novel. Choice B notes a thematic overlap without introducing Grace. Choice D describes the novel but doesn’t name Grace as its author.
